ab: Manga’ay kako a milicay tisowan haw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Manga’ayto, mangalayay kako.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Manga’ay kako a milicay tisowan haw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Na’ay, caay kahakowa ko kafana’an ako.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: Cilimaw kiso to herek no lahok haw? manga’ay a liso’en ako kiso?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Nga’ayto, talaen ako kiso i loma’.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: Cilimaw kiso ano papacem haw? Sapilicayan kako to pakayniay tono to’asan a kitoh no ‘Amis.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於阿美族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the Amis.

ab: Talalotok kako anopapacem, awa kako i loma’.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: Mafana’ kiso to pinangan no rayray no ’Amis, saan ko katengilan ako, saka sapilicayan kako i tisowananan.
ch: A:我聽說您對阿美族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about Amis traditions and customs, so I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: Caay ka hakowa ko kafana’an ako, kawrira paca’of han ako ko licay iso.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: Manga’ay kako a misasing to dademakay a sasing haw? O saka narikayaw a pakaharateng to ikor.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Nga’ayto, mamaan aca saw.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Hakowaayto ko mihecaan iso?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Siwaayto a polo’ ira ko falo ko mihecaan ako.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: Masaka^mangho kiso.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: Cakira, malitengto kako.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: O nanicowaay kiso?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: O nani Fata’ananay kako.
en: B: I’m from [Matthew’s Saddle]. *Please change [Matthew Saddle] to an ethnic location.

ab: O ’Amis ko fafahi iso?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是阿美族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also [Amis]?

ab: Caay, o Piyuma cingra.
en: B: No, she is a Puyuma.

ab: Mafana’ kiso a caciyaw to romaroma a sowal haw?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: Mafana’ kako a somowal to no ’Amis ato no Piyuma.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和卑南語。
en: B: I can speak Ami and Puyuma.

ab: Malipahak ko faloco’ a miliso’ i tisowan, o maan ko kapaysinan no mi’adopay?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: Nga’ay to, yo kapahho kako, mararid kako a talalotok a mi’adop.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: Aray tisowanan, tadamanga’ay ko pipakafana’ iso anini a romi’ad.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: Sahto o so’linay koni sowalan ako, mahaen ko kitoh no tato’asan.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: Tahini ko kalaholol ita, tadamiahowid to pipalada’ iso.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: Aka ka ngodo, liyaw sato a malaholol haca kita.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: Miliyasto kako, nanay miliyaw a pakatengilho to adihayay a kitoh.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Nga’ayto! Na’onen ko rakat.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
